across.clue: if a liquid flows it moves smoothly and continuously in one direction.
to make something happen usually something bad.
to stop something from moving through or along something else.
someone that you do a particular activity with
the way that someone or something behaves.
a substance that can flow has no fixed shape and is not a solid or gas.
an idea problem situation etc that you discuss or write about
An amount of time during which something happens.
a piece of evidence that something is happening or that something exists.
a variety of objects or things.

down.clue: the roads or paths that you use when you go from one place to another.
something that you do for someone in order to help them
a company that owns aircraft and takes people or goods by plane from one place to another.
A talk to a group of people about a particular subject especially at a college or university.
To move information to your computer from a computer system or the Internet.
to move closer to someone or something.
to disappear below the surface of the water.
people in general living together in organised communities with laws and traditions controlling the way they behave towards one another
if a ball or other object bounces or if you bounce it it hits a surface then immediately moves away from it.
to show something by holding out your finger or a long thin object.
